message:
feat(messaging): implement ProtocolAdapter trait and RespProtocolAdapter

Replaces the empty ProtocolAdapterPlaceholder stub with a full protocol
adapter contract covering capability negotiation, encode/decode message
framing, connection lifecycle management, and health-check reporting.

New types:
- ProtocolAdapter trait: the core abstraction with negotiate(),
  encode(), decode(), on_connect/on_disconnect(), health_check()
- ProtocolAdapterError: typed error enum for negotiation, encoding,
  decoding, connection, and health failures
- ProtocolConnectionState: tracks connection phase (Disconnected,
  Negotiating, Connected, Draining, Failed)
- ProtocolTransportEvent: models events flowing through the adapter
- ProtocolCapabilities/ProtocolNegotiation/ProtocolHealth: metadata
  types for the negotiation and monitoring surfaces

RespProtocolAdapter: a concrete implementation backed by the existing
Redis RESP2 wire types. Supports RESP2 bulk-string and array encoding,
connection state tracking, and basic health reporting.

redis.rs: RespValue::try_decode and try_decode_with_limits are now pub
so the adapter can call them from outside the module.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
